approvals.owner_message:
Findings from your current session point to a code-path issue, not just a missing install path.

The failed run record shows Alibre launch failed after tool execution, with only list operations succeeding: interactions.jsonl:65.
A prior run did discover an Alibre executable path artifact under Program, but still ended failed: interactions.jsonl:44.
Recovery logic currently hardcodes a specific Alibre version folder and even includes a bogus exe candidate built from user text: admin.py:572 and admin.py:573.
The executable launcher marks failure if the initial process exits quickly, which is common for bootstrap launchers that spawn a child and exit: tools.py:189.
Planner fallback also assumes narrow executable naming/location patterns: planner.py:167 and planner.py:175.
Use this prompt with your bot:

Please implement a robust fix for Alibre launch failures in desktop_execution.

Context:

Recent failed interactions are in interactions.jsonl lines 44 and 65.
Current recovery in admin.py incorrectly hardcodes Alibre path/version and includes a garbage candidate exe name ("be sure to save the alibre executable location for future calls.exe").
launch_executable in tools.py can false-fail when bootstrap exe exits quickly but child process stays running.
Required changes:

Remove brittle and unsafe recovery candidate construction
In _infer_execution_recovery_tool_calls, delete any user-text-derived exe candidate strings.
Remove hardcoded version path assumptions.
Never generate executable names from arbitrary owner message text.
Add deterministic Alibre executable resolution
Create a resolver method in admin orchestrator (or shared utility) that:
First checks a persisted cache file in state (for example state/alibre_executable_path.json) and validates it exists.
Searches standard install roots for both names:
AlibreDesign.exe
Alibre Design.exe
Searches both Program Files and Program Files (x86), including versioned Alibre Design folders and Program subfolders.
Returns structured result: found path, discovery method, attempted paths.
On success, persist the resolved path for future calls.
Improve recovery action for desktop_execution Alibre requests
Prefer launch_executable with resolved absolute path.
If launch_executable fails, run one bounded fallback run_command using Start-Process with the resolved path.
Return clear diagnostics (attempted path, exception text, exit code if available).
Fix launch_executable false negatives
In _tool_launch_executable, do not fail only because parent process exits within wait window.
If parent exits early, verify whether Alibre is running by process-name check (for example AlibreDesign or Alibre) before declaring failure.
Keep current success payload, but include resolved path and verification method.
On failure, raise actionable error text with path and verification checks.
Add tests
Add/extend tests in test_admin_orchestrator.py to cover:
Recovery call generation for Alibre does not include arbitrary user-text exe names.
Resolver can find versioned Program path and cache it.
launch_executable treats quick parent exit + running child as success.
Failure path emits useful diagnostics.
Update existing tests that currently only assert Start-Process string existence to assert new behavior.
Acceptance criteria:

A request like "please launch Alibre CAD and send me a screenshot" produces a plan that resolves a real exe path and attempts launch with launch_executable.
No hardcoded single version folder remains in Alibre recovery logic.
No user message fragments are converted to executable filenames.
Tests pass for the new launch and recovery behavior.
Output format:

Provide changed files list.
Provide short rationale per file.
Provide exact pytest command run and its result.
